rejection_reason: Rule C is a coarse year-level approximation of the listing-timing test and provides no methodological advantage over the exact-date Rule B. It is retained only as a documented, rejected readiness candidate and must never appear as a final canonical eligibility flag.
date_semantics_note: These dates are the first observed trading dates from the official TSETMC API. They are NOT IPO dates, admission dates, or listing dates.
notes: Rule A is the primary eligibility rule for the main financial-statement sample. Rule B is a listing-timing robustness rule and must not replace Rule A in the primary sample. Rule C must not appear as a final canonical eligibility flag; it may remain documented only as a rejected readiness candidate. Later market-feature work must define a separate minimum trading-history or market-lookback rule; Rule B must not be treated as that future market-data rule. This approval is explicit user/data-owner confirmation supported by the completed Stage124 Gate B readiness scientific comparison; no external reviewer approval is claimed. Modeling, tuning, SHAP, SMOTE, calibration, temporal splitting, feature selection, and article result generation remain prohibited.
